textInfo.title: Moderate Cardiovascular Risk Inflammation Marker
textInfo.medicalDescription: hs-CRP level is 2.6 mg/l, which falls into the range for 'mittleres KHK Risiko' (medium coronary heart disease risk) of 1.0 - 3.0 mg/l as per the lab report's interpretation.

textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your C-reactive protein (CRP) level is 2.6 mg/l. This protein is a marker for inflammation in the body. Your result falls into a range that is associated with a moderate risk of future cardiovascular issues. It's a useful piece of information for managing your long-term heart health.

textInfo.title: Increased Fat in Right Psoas Major Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: The intramuscular fat (IMF) of the right psoas major muscle is 15.3%, which is above the normal range of 4.5% to 7.1%. This indicates an intermediate level of fatty infiltration.
textInfo.originalDescription: biomarker_name: IMF_psoas_major right, value: 15.3, unit: %, minNormalValue: 4.5, maxNormalValue: 7.1
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The amount of fat within your right psoas major muscle, a deep muscle in your lower back, is higher than the optimal range. This is known as intramuscular fat infiltration. Maintaining healthy muscle quality is important for strength and overall metabolic health.

textInfo.title: Cyst-like changes in the right hip socket
textInfo.medicalDescription: Zentral im rechten Acetabulum axial 3,2 x 2,5 cm grosse multizystische Veränderung subkortikal/subkortikal, DD subkortikale Zysten bei möglichem Knorpeldefekt, DD anderweitige Läsion von benignem Aspekt.
textInfo.originalDescription: Zentral im rechten Acetabulum axial 3,2 x 2,5 cm grosse multizystische Veränderung subkortikal/subkortikal,
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI revealed a multi-chambered, cyst-like area in your right hip socket (acetabulum), measuring 3.2 by 2.5 centimeters. This change is located just beneath the hard outer layer of the bone. The radiologist suggests two main possibilities, both of which appear benign (non-cancerous). One is that these are cysts that have formed due to a possible defect or wear in the nearby cartilage. Another possibility is a different type of benign lesion. The overall appearance is not concerning for a serious condition.

textInfo.title: Disc bulge in the neck without nerve impact
textInfo.medicalDescription: Wirbelsäule: Diskusprotrusion HWK 5/6 ohne Hinweis auf Nervenwurzelaffektion.
textInfo.originalDescription: Wirbelsäule: Diskusprotrusion HWK 5/6 ohne Hinweis auf Nervenwurzelaffektion.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows a disc protrusion in your neck at the level between the fifth and sixth cervical vertebrae (C5/C6). A disc protrusion means that the soft, gel-like center of the spinal disc is bulging outwards, similar to a cushion being slightly squashed. Importantly, the report notes that this bulge is not pressing on or affecting any of the nearby nerve roots, which is a positive finding.

textInfo.title: Elevated Monocytes
textInfo.medicalDescription: Patient's monocyte percentage is elevated (10.5%), outside the reference range of 2.0-9.5%. This condition is known as monocytosis and can be associated with chronic inflammation, infection, or other underlying conditions.

textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your blood test shows a higher than normal percentage of monocytes, a type of white blood cell. This can be a sign that your body is fighting off a chronic infection or inflammation. While often temporary, it's a finding worth monitoring.

textInfo.title: Low Iron Stores (Ferritin)
textInfo.medicalDescription: Ferritin level is low at 18.4 µg/l (reference range: 30-200 µg/l). The report notes this range (15-30 µg/l) indicates 'leere oder knappe Eisenspeicher' (empty or low iron stores), consistent with iron deficiency.
textInfo.originalDescription: Ferritin (Siemens) ' L 18.4 µg/l 30-200. 15-30 µg/l: leere oder knappe Eisenspeicher.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your ferritin level is below the normal range. Ferritin is a protein that stores iron in your body, so a low level indicates that your body's iron reserves are depleted. This can lead to fatigue and other symptoms of iron deficiency.

textInfo.title: Elevated Lipase
textInfo.medicalDescription: Lipase level is elevated at 96 U/l, which is above the reference range of <53 U/l. Elevated lipase is a key indicator for pancreatic inflammation (pancreatitis) or other pancreatic conditions.
textInfo.originalDescription: Lipase ' H 96 U/I <53
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your lipase level is higher than the normal range. Lipase is an enzyme produced by the pancreas to help digest fats. An elevated level can be a sign of inflammation or stress on the pancreas and should be discussed with a doctor.

textInfo.title: Simple, common cysts found in the liver
textInfo.medicalDescription: Leber: Mehrere einfache kleine homogene Leberparenchymzysten (bis zu 16 mm), ansonsten Parenchym regelrecht.
textInfo.originalDescription: Leber: Mehrere einfache kleine homogene Leberparenchymzysten (bis zu 16 mm), ansonsten Parenchym regelrecht.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows several small, simple cysts within your liver tissue, with the largest measuring up to 16 mm. These are described as 'simple' and 'homogeneous,' meaning they are thin-walled, fluid-filled sacs that appear uniform. Such cysts are very common, typically benign (non-cancerous), and usually do not cause any symptoms. The rest of your liver tissue was found to be normal.

textInfo.title: Small amount of normal fluid in the pelvis
textInfo.medicalDescription: Freie Flüssigkeit: Wenig pelvin, DD physiologisch
textInfo.originalDescription: Freie Flüssigkeit: Wenig pelvin, DD physiologisch
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The MRI scan of your pelvis shows a small amount of free fluid. The radiologist suggests this is most likely a 'physiological' finding, meaning it is normal and expected. For women, a small amount of fluid in the pelvis is common and often related to the menstrual cycle.

textInfo.medicalDescription: Patient is female and reports a natural, very regular menstrual cycle. Last menstrual period was on 2025-10-23. Patient denies any significant premenstrual or menstrual symptoms.
textInfo.originalDescription: isWoman: yes, hasNaturalCycle: yes, lastPeriodDate: 2025-10-23, cycleRegularity: very_regular, hasSymptoms: no
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: You have a natural and very regular menstrual cycle. Your last period began on October 23, 2025, and you don't experience significant symptoms like pain or mood swings.

textInfo.title: Normal variation in the position of the aorta
textInfo.medicalDescription: Vaskuläre Strukturen: Rechtsseitiger Aortenbogen als Normvariante
textInfo.originalDescription: Vaskuläre Strukturen: Rechtsseitiger Aortenbogen als Normvariante
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The scan of your chest revealed that your aortic arch, the large blood vessel that carries blood from your heart, curves over the right side of your windpipe instead of the more common left side. This is known as a 'right-sided aortic arch' and is considered a normal anatomical variation. It is not a disease and typically does not cause any symptoms or health problems.

textInfo.title: Low Iron Stores (Ferritin)
textInfo.medicalDescription: Ferritin value is 18.4 µg/l, which is below the reference range of 30-200. The lab report adds the interpretation: '15-30 µg/l: leere oder knappe Eisenspeicher' (empty or low iron stores).
textInfo.originalDescription: Ferritin (Siemens) : L 18.4 µg/l
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your ferritin level is low at 18.4 µg/l (normal: 30-200 µg/l). Ferritin is a protein that stores iron, so a low level indicates that your body's iron reserves are depleted. This can lead to iron-deficiency anemia if not addressed.

textInfo.title: Slightly Elevated Eosinophils
textInfo.originalDescription: Eosinophile: 5.2 %
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your eosinophil level, a type of white blood cell, is slightly above the normal range (5.2% vs. a reference of 0.5-5.5%). This can be associated with allergies, asthma, or parasitic infections, but a minor elevation is often not a cause for concern.

textInfo.title: Normal uterine position and healthy ovaries
textInfo.medicalDescription: Kleines Becken / Uterus / Adnexen: Retrovertiert anteflektierter Uterus ohne Hinweis auf pathologische Veränderungen. Adnexen physiologisch.
textInfo.originalDescription: Kleines Becken / Uterus / Adnexen: Retrovertiert anteflektierter Uterus ohne Hinweis auf pathologische Veränderungen. Adnexen physiologisch.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows the position and condition of your uterus and adnexa (ovaries and fallopian tubes). Your uterus is described as 'retroverted and anteflexed,' which refers to its specific tilt and bend within the pelvis. This is a common and normal anatomical variation and not a sign of a problem. The scan showed no signs of abnormal changes in your uterus, and your ovaries and fallopian tubes also appear normal.
